entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Web IDEの画面に表示されているJava Build Pack1廃止の警告の件について
entry.content:
以下のいくつかの情報にあるとおり、SAP Java Buildpack1の削除の期限を、2025年6月末から、10月1日に延期しました。
こちらのNote等にも記載されているとおり、移行作業を行わぬまま10月1日を迎えSAP Java Buildpack 1が削除された後に再デプロイやrestageを実行するとCloud Foundryのアプリケーションが動かなくなります。
Web IDEのツールの問題ではなく、BTPのCloud Foundry RuntimeでSAP Java Buildpack 1が削除されるという話ですね


これ以上の延期は多分難しいと思いますので、10/1までSAP Java Buildpack 2へ移行することをお勧めします。


https://me.sap.com/notes/0003625132
https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/new-migrate-your-applications-from-sap-java-buildpack-v-1-to-v-2

entry.title: How to Configure QS41 & QS51: Steps for Opening Customizing Views in Different Systems
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’d like to share a quick guide based on a recent configuration we performed to open QS41 and QS51 for customizing in different systems (Development, Quality, and Production). This can help those facing restrictions while accessing QS41/QS51 catalog maintenance across landscapes.

🔧For Development & Quality Systems

SAP Note: 356483

To open the configuration of QS41/QS51, follow these steps:

  1. Execute transaction SE54.

  2. Enter the following customizing objects one by one:

    • V_QPGR_KAT

    • V_QPCD_KAT

    • V_QPAC_KAT

    • V_QPAM_KAT

i. Choose "Generated Objects".

ii. Click on "Create/Change".

iii. On the Generation Environment screen, scroll to the "Dialog Data Transport Details" block at the bottom.

iv. Select "No, or user, recording routine".

v. Save your changes and capture in TR.

⚠️Important: Do not transport these changes to the production system. These settings are system-specific and must be applied only where necessary (i.e., Dev or QA).

🏭For Production System

SAP Note: 736920

  1. Execute transaction SOBJ.

  2. Choose the 'Enter Conditions' indicator.

  3. Click 'Maintain'.

i. Specify and update the following objects one by one:

V_QPAM_CL

V_QPGR_CL

ii. Set the indicator 'Current Settings'.

iii. Save the changes and capture in TR.

Follow the same procedure in the Development system, create the Transport Request (TR), and then move the TR to Quality and Production.

✅Summary:

This setup ensures QS41/QS51 catalog maintenance is accessible and editable where required without compromising system integrity. 

Let me know if you’ve used alternate approaches or encountered specific scenarios during implementation. Your inputs are most welcome!

Best regards,
Binod Kumar

entry.title: Re: Sunset of BEx Tools - Frequently Asked Questions and Answers (FAQ)
entry.content:
Hi Vincent,

In order to identify the list of users still using BEx Analyzer, go totransaction SE16, and table RSDDSTAT_OLAP:

  • For BEx Analyzer / Workbook usage:
    • Use selection “BEX” as STEPTP field and “3200”  as EVENTID.
    • Number of entries > 0 show the usage of BEx Analyzer / Workbooks in the system.
  • The USERID field in the results will identify which users are actively using BEx tools

If you export the USERID list from here and select the distinct entries, you will have all user ids who are still using BEx Aaalyzer.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: How to identify the list of users using SAP bex analy...
entry.content:
Hi Vincent,

In order to identify the list of users still using BEx Analyzer, go totransaction SE16, and table RSDDSTAT_OLAP:

For BEx Analyzer / Workbook usage: Use selection “BEX” as STEPTP field and “3200” as EVENTID. Number of entries > 0 show the usage of BEx Analyzer / Workbooks in the system.

The USERID field in the results will identify which users are actively using BEx tools.

If you export the USERID list from here and select the distinct entries, you will have all user ids who are still using BEx Aaalyzer. Hope this helps.

entry.title: i want to merge the dulplicate values in sap.m.table and align them to center as shown in image
entry.content:
The table has to be formatted as above image and below is the table which i have removed duplicates using  mergeDuplicates="true"

view.xml

<mvc:View
  controllerName="formscoring.controller.View1"
  xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
  xmlns="sap.m">
  
  <Page id="page" title="Supplier Quality Monthly Scoring">
    <content>
      <Table id="SupplierTable" items="{supplier>/SupplierScoring}" inset="false" >
        <columns>
          <Column hAlign="Center" mergeDuplicates="true" class="centerVertical"><Text text="Category" /></Column>
          <Column hAlign="Center" mergeDuplicates="true" class="centerVertical"><Text text="Item" /></Column>
          <Column hAlign="Center" mergeDuplicates="true" class="centerVertical"><Text text="Performance Metric" /></Column>
          <Column hAlign="Center" mergeDuplicates="true" class="centerVertical"><Text text="Criteria / Weightage" /></Column>
          <Column hAlign="Center" mergeDuplicates="true" class="centerVertical"><Text text="Max Score" /></Column>
          <Column hAlign="Center" class="centerVertical"><Text text="Score" /></Column>
          <Column hAlign="Center" class="centerVertical"><Text text="Remarks / Justification" /></Column>
        </columns>
        <items>
          <ColumnListItem   >
            <cells >
              <Text class="centerVertical" text="{supplier>Category}"  />
              <Text class="centerVertical" text="{supplier>Item}" />
              <Text class="centerVertical" text="{supplier>PerformanceMetric}" />
              <Text class="centerVertical" text="{supplier>Criteria}" />
              <Text class="centerVertical" text="{supplier>MaxScore}" />
              <Text class="centerVertical" text="{supplier>Score}" />
              <Text class="centerVertical" text="{supplier>Remarks}" />
            </cells>
          </ColumnListItem>
        </items>
      </Table>
    </content>
  </Page>
</mvc:View>
 
.centerVertical {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  height: 100%;
  justify-content: center;
  text-align: center;
}

entry.title: How to Handle Currency Decimal Places in Amount Conversion and Reporting in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello.

I have a question regarding currency conversion for amounts.

I use KRW, which has zero decimal places. For example, when I enter 1,000 in the SAP system, it is stored internally as 10.00. Therefore, in the reporting layer, I multiply the amount fields by 100.

The problem is that if there are hundreds of amount fields, I have to repeat this calculation hundreds of times in the script.

,CASE WHEN "REC_WAERS" = 'KRW' THEN "VV201_F" * 100 ELSE "VV201_F" END AS "VV201_F"
,CASE WHEN "REC_WAERS" = 'KRW' THEN "VV201_T" * 100 ELSE "VV201_T" END AS "VV201_T"
,CASE WHEN "REC_WAERS" = 'KRW' THEN "VV201_V" * 100 ELSE "VV201_V" END AS "VV201_V"


What are the available methods for handling currency decimal places in Datasphere or a CDS View, and what is the simplest way to manage this?


I would also appreciate it if you could share some typical use cases for how this is handled in practice.

entry.title: Android App Crashing with Object Card in Tab, and Bottom Navigation (MDK 25.6)
entry.content:
Hello Everyone

We are encountering a critical issue with our SAP MDK Android application, built using MDK SDK version 25.6. The app is frequently crashing, specifically when using Object Card controls in the tab and bottom navigation. We've also observed crashes on some seemingly "normal" pages that uses Object Card controls.

The crashes are significantly impacting the app's usability and we are struggling to identify the root cause. We have tried the following without success:

  • Rebuilding the custom client.

  • Testing on different Android devices and OS versions.

Specific Scenarios where crashes occur:

  • Object Card Pages: When displaying pages containing Object Card collections, particularly upon initial load or when interacting with the cards (e.g., scrolling, pressing).

  • Tab Navigation: When switching between tabs in a tabbed page.

  • Bottom Navigation: When navigating using the bottom navigation bar.

  • General Pages: Sporadic crashes on other pages, although less frequent than those involving object cards or navigation.

Any guidance, suggestions, or potential solutions would be highly appreciated.

entry.title: BPA : Maintaining Approver Team Members and Selection
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are working on implementation of a standard BPA Template Fixed Asset Write off Approval.

We have a query regarding the optimized way to maintain Approval ids.

As per the requirement, the number of “Approval Levels” and “Approver Group” in each Level will be decided based on the Net Amount and Disposal Category.

We have designed the Decision Table accordingly to select the Approver Group.

But, we want to ensure that maintenance of users in approver groups can be done without need to touch the BPA object repeatedly.

As we understand, Decision table cannot be maintained directly in non-development environments.

We are exploring following methods and would like to highlight the challenges we are facing in implementing them,

  1. Cloud Identity Service,
    • How it will work:
      1.  We can Create Groups and maintain the users in each group  
    •  
    •  Challenges:
      1. Is it possible to create multiple variants of groups based on attributes like Country or Company Code?
      2. If we can, how to filter it in run time based on the actual data being processed in an instance?
      3. Can we use Group for sending only information Email Notifications to outlook (It only accepts mail id and no Group input available)?
  2. Responsibility Management,
    • How it will work :
      1.       A team is created containing Team members and each member is assigned an approver group
      2.       Attributes are added to team, which will help to select the team to be used based on country / company code  
    • Challenges:
      1.       Need to make API call to get Responsibility Management data
      2.       It is primarily designed for workflows on S/4, is it ok to use it with BPA application?
  3. Process Flexibility Cockpit,
    1. Is it possible to use it with BPA?
    2. If yes, can it be used to maintain the Decision Table directly?

 

Sharing an example of possible Approver Levels and corresponding Approver Groups for Single Country for your refence,

 

Value

Approval levels and Groups

(A : Approval Task, N : Only Notification)

< €500k

No Approval required

≥ €500k

1) APG1 (A), 2) APG2 (N)

≥€500k ≥€2 M

1) APG1 (A), 2) APG2 (N), 3) APG3 (A)

≥€2 M 

1) APG1 (A), 2) APG2 (N), 3) APG3 (A), 4) APG4 (N)

 

Thanks,

Nitin

entry.title: Re: Custom Code decommission - Transaction usage
entry.content: ST03N will only count an object as used when it is actually executed. When Transactions are loaded into memory but not executed yet, they will not be counted by ST03N. Please check more detailed explanation in below KBA:3113122 - Custom Code PROG is missing Last Usage information "Resolution In the case of a report that means: 1) Starting the report (e.g. press F8 in SE80) causes - that the selection screen (dynpro) is generated - the selection screen (dynpro) is shown - the ABAP report is loaded into memory (triggering SBLM) - but no single line of ABAP code was called/executed to this point. (Unless you have defined a load-of-program event in the report) 2) Executing the report (e.g. press F8 when your selection screen is visible for inputs) triggers the execution of the start-of-selection part in the report, which is an actual call of ABAP code and therefore triggers SCMON log-writing. The assumption is that a user started the transaction/report but did not actually execute it. Therefore no ABAP code was actually called/executed and therefore it would be correct that SCMON did not write a record."

entry.title: How to change the Rating Calculation in Performance and 360° Forms (Need Mean Calculation)?
entry.content:
We would like to change the rating calculation method in Performance and 360° forms where behaviors are evaluated. Currently, the rating calculation does not use a classical average (mean), but instead follows the methodology described in  KBA 2642965.

The current calculation method, which excludes ratings of 0 or "no rating," does not align with the needs of certain organizations. Specifically, the exclusion of these ratings results in an incomplete and potentially skewed evaluation. A classical mean calculation, where ratings of 0 are included in the total count, would provide a more accurate and comprehensive performance assessment.

Is there any way to adjust the rating calculation method?

SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals

Hello, thank you for reaching out.
The PIR can only be sent when the vendor has been synchronized from S/4 to SLP and from there to Sourcing, the vendor is active, and has a SAP Vendor ID. Even if the supplier can be invited to an event, a PIR cannot be generated until the synch is done. Hence please make sure supplier has been synchronized and try to send the PIR again.

entry.title: Re: Dual control sensitive fields for vendor master in sap
entry.content:
Dear Mohammed Nafees,

  • Confirmation of changes on sensitive fields in customer/vendor master data is required during automatic payment run and cheque printing. There is no need to confirm the customer/vendor changes during invoice posting/manual payment.
  • The system only checks unconfirmed changes if online payments with forms (for example outgoing checks) are created during manual process. This is not the case in "normal" manual posting and clearing transactions, so that unconfirmed changes are not checked.

Kindly refer this KBA : Dual Control Sensitive Fields for Vendor Master in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: Purpose Codes in Payment Media in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Dear Merle,

there is no need to enter the Payment Reason into both Business Partner AND invoice. One source is enough.

It is difficult to find out the issue without looking at your settings, but make sure that there are no invalid conditions anywhere above the element where you are mapping the Purpose Code. You can use the Trace Tool to verify that:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/analyze-the-processing-of-outgoing-payment-files-from-map-format-data/ba-p/13548449

Also make sure that:

1. You are using the same Application Area on your Payment Format and in the Purpose Codes mapping table

2. You are mapping the correct field (FPAYHX-SND_CTRY_CODE / FPAYHX-REC_CTRY_CODE)

Kind regards,

Ondrej

entry.title: Re: Session 5 Recap & Highlights - Enterprise Architects guide on SAP BW Modernization
entry.content:
@appel_solar_dk  - Thank you for your candid feedback—it’s truly appreciated. You’re absolutely right that simply moving BW to the cloud doesn’t automatically equate to modernization, especially when the underlying core remains rooted in decades-old architecture.  That said, the intent with this session as twofold: first, to help organizations get clarity on transition paths (whether to BW/4HANA or DataSphere), and second, to introduce architectural thinking around decoupling, governance, and data productization. 

Hopefully, upcoming sessions in the series will push the envelope a bit further, particularly around innovation layers (like semantic integration or AI-driven orchestration), rather than just platform repositioning. Your feedback definitely helps steer the dialogue in that direction.

cc: @JaSoN_Luo

entry.title: Requirement to Implement Material-Based PR Release Strategy (2-Level / 3-Level)
entry.content:
1️⃣ Define characteristics

  • Create a characteristic (e.g. ZMAT_CHECK) of type CHAR / NUM as needed.
  • The value of this characteristic will represent whether the material code exists in the table or not. Example:
    • Y → Exists → 3-level
    • N → Not exists → 2-level

2️⃣ Create a custom table

  • A Z-table (e.g. ZPR_MAT_CHECK) where you maintain material codes that require 3-level release.

MATNR

10000001

10000002

10000003

3️⃣ Enhance the release strategy determination

  • In user-exit / BAdI (like MEREQ001 or MEREQ_BADI_RELEASE_STRATEGY for PR):
    • Write logic to check if PR material code exists in ZPR_MAT_CHECK.
    • If exists → set characteristic ZMAT_CHECK = Y.
    • If not exists → set ZMAT_CHECK = N.

4️⃣ Release strategy setup

  • Maintain two release strategies:
    • Strategy 1 (2-level) → When ZMAT_CHECK = N
    • Strategy 2 (3-level) → When ZMAT_CHECK = Y

  e_characteristics-zmat_check = 'Y'. " 3-level

  e_characteristics-zmat_check = 'N'. " 2-level

 

 

 

Z-table

 

Field Name

Data Element

Data Type

Length

Key

Description

MATNR

MATNR

CHAR

18

✔

Material Number

 

Important Notes:

 

  1. The table check is performed:
    • When PR is created
    • When PR is changed
    • When release strategy is simulated

entry.title: Re: Multiple Prices for a product
entry.content:
Hello @candidochris 

Thank you for your question

Managing multiple price types like Purchase Cost, Retail Price, or Jobber Price isn’t handled through classic price lists in S4/HANA public cloud, like in Business One.

Instead, common options are,

  • Purchase Info Records for supplier-specific purchase prices
  • Condition Records using pricing procedures for sales-side prices (Retail, Jobber, etc.)
  • For non-transactional prices (e.g., Minimum-Advertised Price), these can be stored as custom fields or in a custom business object if needed

Standard purchase and sales prices are managed via Info Records and Condition Records
Additional prices like Retail or MAP can be handled with custom fields or extensions

There’s no standard mass price list functionality like B1, but pricing can be managed via conditions, master data extensions, or APIs depending on your process.

You can check below SAP Help documentation for your references
SAP Help – Pricing in S/4HANA Public Cloud

Recommended SAP Learning Resources:

 SAP Learning Journey – S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Sourcing & Procurement
Includes how to manage Purchase Info Records, supplier pricing, and master data relevant for purchase-side price handling

 SAP Learning Journey – S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Sales Pricing
Covers condition records, pricing procedures, and setup for customer pricing

SAP Learning Journey – Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
Shows how to handle custom fields, custom business objects, or additional price attributes not covered by standard pricing

Hope it helps

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: SAP 分析云经验分享：如何批量修改日历流程被分派者
entry.content:
SAP 分析云经验分享：如何批量修改日历流程被分派者 

一、介绍 

在当今数字化驱动、企业追求卓越运营的时代，SAP 分析云作为一款功能强大的综合性工具，正全方位助力企业提升决策效能与管理秩序。它承载着数据可视化、预测分析、规划以及协作等多重使命，为企业勾勒出一幅智能管理的宏伟蓝图。 

然而，随着企业版图的拓展、业务流程的精细化以及团队协作的深度化，我们对 SAP 分析云各项功能的期许也在不断攀升。聚焦于 SAP 分析云中的日历功能，尽管它在事件安排、流程追踪等方面表现出色，但目前的筛选机制存在一定的局限性，筛选涉及事件相关人员时，仅能依据 “我的角色” 进行筛选，难以满足企业多元且复杂的管理诉求。 

试想这样的情境：当员工 A 因休假暂别岗位，事件 1 的流程负责人 B 想要迅速梳理出该事件下，由 A 负责且尚未完成的子任务，并重新调配人力，却因为筛选功能的限制，操作时会遇到一些困难；当员工 A 离职，日历管理员肩负起在海量日历数据中，逐一查找 A 身影并进行批量替换的重任，面对冗长且繁琐的操作流程，效率有所降低。为了解决这一痛点，我开发了一个使用了日历API脚本的故事应用。这篇文章中，我将为大家详细介绍。 

二、如何使用“批量修改日历流程被分派者”应用 

使用该故事应用前可在该故事页面查看相关故事应用的使用说明。 

1、要执行此操作，您需要具备以下权限： 

  • 分析模型的“读”权限 
  • 公共文件的“管理”权限 
  • 私有文件的“读”权限 

2、请按照以下步骤获取父日历流程的事件 ID： 

  • 点击导航栏中的“日历”，并选择您要编辑的事件。 

  • 当事件页面打开时，复制 浏览器地址栏中URL中“eventId=” 后面的部分以获取事件 ID。 

或者： 

  • 选中事件后，点击右上角的“详细信息”，在弹出菜单栏的右上角，点击“更多”，然后点击“事件设置”。 

  • 如果未勾选“允许外部 API 访问”选项，请勾选该选项。 

 

  • 然后点击“显示详情”以查看并复制事件 ID。 

3、将事件 ID 粘贴到文本框中。 

4、使用下拉菜单选择您要替换的被分派者。 

5、使用下拉菜单选择您要添加的新被分派者。 

6、点击 “Confirm and Replace” 按钮。 

  • 对于替换成功的事件，系统会弹出绿色窗口以提示替换完成。 
  • 任务全部完成后，系统会弹出灰色窗口，提示“Finished processing all processes and tasks”表示任务已全部完成。 

  • 如果在替换整个流程的过程中存在未被替换成功的事件，则会提示未替换成功的事件数量以及对应的事件ID和事件名称。 

三、注意事项 

1、仅可在父流程中使用，不支持其他类型的日历事件。如果在输入框中输入非流程事件的事件ID，如常规任务的事件ID，则会提示“Only events with the event type of "Process" can be modified. Please enter the Event ID of an event with the event type of "Process".” 

2、无法替换状态为“已取消、失败、成功或部分成功”的任务的指派对象。 

3、无法替换数据锁定任务、数据操作任务、多操作任务、输入任务或发布任务的指派对象。 

四、代码实现 

接下来将对主要的实现逻辑进行介绍 。Calendar提供了比较丰富的集成API ，通过利用相关 API 可实现以上功能。主要逻辑步骤如下： 

1、变量初始化与界面设置 

在实现该功能之前，需要在故事应用中配置“日历集成”，并完成变量的初始化。以下是部分关键变量的说明： 

RemovedAssignee // 想要替换的被分派者 
AddedAssignee // 想要添加的被分派者 
calendarEventId // 相关日历的事件 ID 
removeAssigneeFilterSelection // 下拉菜单实现移除assignee的脚本变量 
addAssigneeFilterSelection // 下拉菜单实现添加assignee的脚本变量   
count // 用来记录没有替换成功的事件的数量 
arrayFailedEvent  // 替换失败的事件集合（事件Id） 
arrayFailedEventName //替换失败的时间集合（事件名称）

同时，可以对加载状态的呈现方式和系统消息提示类型进行相应的预设与配置。 

2、获取事件对象并进行校验 

首先，根据 calendarEventId 获取对应的事件对象 task，并对其存在性进行校验。随后判断该事件的类型是否为“流程类型”（Process）：若类型不符，则提示用户仅支持对“流程类型”的事件进行操作，并中止后续执行。 

var task = calIntegration1.getCalendarEventById(calendarEventId); 
if(task === undefined){ 
Application.showMessage( 
ApplicationMessageType.Warning, 
"The event ID you entered is incorrect. Please verify and re-enter it." 
); 
//用来控制加载状态   
   Application.hideBusyIndicator(); 
return; 
} 
//添加一个判断 判断用户输入事件的事件类型是流程还是其他     
if(task.getType() !== CalendarTaskType.Process ){ 
Application.showMessage( 
ApplicationMessageType.Warning, 
"Only events with the event type of \"Process\" can be modified. Please enter the Event ID of an event with the event type of \"Process.\"" 
); 
//用来控制加载状态    
Application.hideBusyIndicator(); 
return; 
}

3、处理根流程任务 

将事件对象转换为 CalendarProcess 类型。 

对根流程执行被分派者移除（removeAssignee）操作。 

  • 如果移除成功，则执行添加新负责人（addAssignee）。 
  • 根据操作成功与否，输出对应的提示。 
  • 如果失败，记录失败信息并加入失败数组。 
var task_process = cast(Type.CalendarProcess,task); 
// 创建队列并初始化（使用数组模拟队列） 
var eventQueue = ArrayUtils.create(Type.CalendarProcess);  
// 将根流程加入队列 
eventQueue.push(task_process);  
var assigneeRemoved_root = task_process.removeAssignee(RemovedAssignee);  
//用来控制加载状态  
Application.hideBusyIndicator();  
//判断根流程是否删除assignee成功 
 if(assigneeRemoved_root) {  
    Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Success, "Assignee has been successfully removeded to Calendar Event " ); 
     //只有根流程assignee删除成功后才会给根流程添加assignee        
      var assigneeAdded_root = task_process.addAssignee(AddedAssignee);     
    //判断根流程是否添加assignee成功     
    if(assigneeAdded_root) {   
           Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Success, "Assignee has been successfully addded to Calendar Event " ); 
     }   
     else {   
           Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Error, "Assignee has failed to be added to Calendar Event "); 
     }  
}   
   else {   
       Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Error, "Assignee has failed to be removed to Calendar Event "); 
       count++; 
       arrayFailedEventName.push(calIntegration1.getCalendarEventById(calendarEventId).getName());  
       arrayFailedEvent.push(calendarEventId); 
   }

4、广度优先遍历所有的子流程和子任务 

对队列 eventQueue 进行广度优先遍历所有子事件。 

对每一个子事件执行： 

  • 获取其对象。 
  • 如果是流程类型，继续加入队列遍历其子项。 
  • 对每个子项尝试移除原被分派者，成功后尝试添加新被分派者。 
  • 成功与否都记录日志和信息，失败的加入失败数组。 
// 广度优先遍历处理所有流程    
while (eventQueue.length > 0) { 
    // 从队列头部取出当前流程     
    var currentProcess = eventQueue.shift(); 
    // 获取当前流程的所有子项（子流程或子任务）  
    var childrenIds = currentProcess.getChildren(); 
    // 遍历所有子项    
    for (var i = 0; i < childrenIds.length; i++) { 
        var childEvent = calIntegration1.getCalendarEventById(childrenIds[i]);  
        // 如果是子流程，加入队列继续处理其子项    
        if (childEvent.getType() === CalendarTaskType.Process) { 
            var childProcess = cast(Type.CalendarProcess, childEvent); 
            eventQueue.push(childProcess); // 将子流程加入队列   
        }     
        // 对当前子项（无论流程还是任务）执行移除操作   
        var assigneeRemoved = childEvent.removeAssignee(RemovedAssignee); 
        // 处理删除操作结果   
        if (assigneeRemoved) { 
            Application.showMessage( 
                ApplicationMessageType.Success, 
                "Assignee removed from: " + childEvent.getId() 
            ); 
           var assigneeAdded = childEvent.addAssignee(AddedAssignee); 
           // 处理添加操作结果     
     if (assigneeAdded) { 
         Application.showMessage( 
         ApplicationMessageType.Success, 
         "Assignee added to: " + childEvent.getId() 
          );  
     } 
     else { 
         Application.showMessage( 
         ApplicationMessageType.Error, 
         "Failed to add to: " + childEvent.getId() 
         ); 
     }  
  }  
       else { 
               count++; 
               arrayFailedEvent.push(childEvent.getId());                                    arrayFailedEventName.push(calIntegration1.getCalendarEventById(childEvent.getId()).getName());  
       Application.showMessage( 
                ApplicationMessageType.Error, 
                "Failed to remove from: " + childEvent.getId() 
                                 ); 
       }    
    } 
}

5、输出错误信息和结果提示 

  •  如果有失败的事件，显示失败数量。 
  • 对每一个失败事件，显示事件名和 ID。 
  • 最后输出“任务处理完成”提示信息。 
var count_string = ConvertUtils.numberToString(count); 
// 判断是否有未替换成功的事件，如果有，输出未替换成功的事件数量   
if(count !== 0){ 
Application.showMessage( 
               ApplicationMessageType.Warning, 
               "You have \"" + count_string + "\" events where the assignee failed to be replaced successfully."  
              ); 
}  
for (var j = 0; j < count; j++) { 
               warningMessage = warningMessage + "The assignee of event  \"" + arrayFailedEventName[j]  + "\" failed to be replaced successfully.\n"; 
               warningMessage = warningMessage + " the event ID: " + arrayFailedEvent[j] + "\n\n"; 
} 
if (warningMessage) { 
               Application.showMessage( 
               ApplicationMessageType.Warning, 
               warningMessage 
              ); 
} 
//任务执行结束   
Application.showMessage( 
              ApplicationMessageType.Info, 
             "Finished processing all processes and tasks" 
);

五、总结 

本文主要分享了我开发“批量修改日历流程被分派者”故事应用的经验。如果您也遇到了类似的问题，希望可以给您启发。

entry.title: New Danish Tax requirements in 2025 and beyond - How SAP can help
entry.content:
Marc Hoessels (PwC Belgium - Managing Director Tax & ERP Transformations) and Anders Strandet Jepsen (PwC Denmark - Partner)

 

  1. Introduction to the Danish Bookkeeping Act

The Danish Bookkeeping Act, in Danish “Bogføringsloven”, outlines the principles and requirements for digital accounting and bookkeeping in Denmark. It includes several immediate tax-related obligations for businesses, such as:

  • Supporting the automatic sending and receiving of e-invoices in OIOUBL/PEPPOL BIS 
  • Enabling the sharing of bookkeeping data in SAF-T format.

Beyond these tax-related requirements, the Act imposes broader bookkeeping obligations, including but not limited to:

  • Performing necessary tax reconciliations for each tax-relevant filing.
  • Registering all company transactions in compliance with bookkeeping standards.
  • Maintaining an audit trail for recorded transactions.
  • Digitally storing purchase and sales invoices.
  • Conducting a full backup of stored data weekly with an independent party inside the EU that meets IT security standards.

These regulations aim to enhance financial transparency, data security, and compliance within Denmark’s digital bookkeeping framework.

Further, the Act distinguishes between approved and non-approved digital bookkeeping systems, where the requirements can differ and the responsibility to comply can lie with either the company or the ERP provider. For non-approved systems, the responsibility lies with the company.

It is therefore important to determine which category one's bookkeeping system belongs to, and which associated requirements must be met and how to meet them. As this could have an impact on when the requirements are/become applicable (depending on the financial year of the entity):

  • 1 July 2024 (past): Digital bookkeeping applies for approved ERP systems
  • 1 January 2025 (present): Digital bookkeeping applies for non-approved ERP systems for most companies in Denmark
  • 1 January 2026 (future): Digital bookkeeping applies to all entities with net annual turnover over DKK 300,000 (approx. EUR 40,000), including foreign companies with branches in Denmark

SAP has clarified that SAP S/4HANA is not officially recognized as a standard bookkeeping system under the Danish Bookkeeping Act. Therefore, it is the client's responsibility to ensure that they are in compliance with the Danish Bookkeeping Act. To help you with this PwC can perform a ‘compliance validation’. This process helps you identify and address any compliance gaps related to the Danish Bookkeeping Act, which encompasses more than just VAT requirements (such as SAF-T, e-invoicing, etc.). As it also includes requirements related to:

  • Ensuring a control trail for accounting data
  • Availability of banking data
  • Reconciliation of banking data
  • Regular back ups of data
  • …

2. E-Invoice compliance under the Act

As mentioned, companies must be able to automatically send and receive e-invoices in Peppol and OIOUBL format. OIOUBL is a Danish e-invoicing standard maintained by the Danish Business Authority and is typically used for transactions with Danish government entities via the NemHandel platform.

Peppol, another accepted e-invoicing format under the Danish Bookkeeping Act, is a globally recognized standard. To use Peppol, companies need a certified Peppol access point before engaging in Peppol e-invoicing. Additionally, it's crucial to ensure that your customers are registered on the Peppol network; otherwise, your e-invoices may not reach their intended destination.

The requirements in the Danish Bookkeeping Act outlines the requirements to system capabilities. The Danish Tax Agency has not (yet) mandated the use for VAT taxable B2B or B2C transactions, but the legislations are expected to align in the near future (likely to be introduced in 2028). 

Next to the strict Danish obligation, also ViDA is around the corner (2030), which impacts your EU cross border transactions with the EU wide implementation of Digital Reporting Requirements (DRR). One aspect of the DRR is to move cross border trade closer to real-time reporting by abolishing the periodic recapitulative statements (e.g. EU sales listings) and instead mandating the suppliers to report the cross border trade within 10 days of the chargeable event. This coincides with the e-invoicing requirements, that are also part of the DRR, where e-invoices is the new default for invoicing and e-invoices must be issued within the same time frame. 

Since more and more countries have implemented, will implement or plan to implement a kind of e-invoicing, it is a good business decision to look at all obligations in the EU and beyond and try to harmonise your e-invoicing solution(s) which will lower your IT cost & maintenance over time. 

3. SAF-T (Standard Audit File for Tax)

The new Bookkeeping Act requires bookkeeping systems to support the SAF-T international standard file format. This facilitates easier and more secure data exchanges between companies, such as with the Danish Tax Agency, and simplifies switching bookkeeping systems.


Currently, companies are not mandated to regularly submit SAF-T files to authorities. However, bookkeeping systems must be capable of generating, loading, and exporting SAF-T files if requested by the Danish Tax Agency, such as during a VAT or tax inspection. The current obligation requires businesses to produce a SAF-T file on header level.

However, this requirement is likely to be extended to all transactions (purchases and sales) by 2028 and with further reporting requirements in the subsequent years.

4. Implementation in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (hereafter SAP DRC) for DK e-invoicing and SAF-T

SAP offers a solution called SAP Document and Reporting Compliance to assist with indirect tax and e-invoicing reporting obligations globally. 

In Denmark, SAP DRC can generate from a reporting perspective:

  • VAT return (where Electronic submission of the VAT return is on the roadmap for delivery)
  • EC Sales Listings
  • The header details of the Danish SAF-T (SAP intends to deliver full SAF-T, once more root elements become mandatory).

From an e-reporting/e-invoicing perspective:

  • Both Peppol and OIOUBL formats via the Peppol Network
  • Peppol e-order
  • Integration to Nemhandel (on the roadmap for delivery).

entry.title: Navigating the Labyrinth: Choosing the Right Persistent Data Storage for Your Custom Development
entry.content:
Hello All,

During a recent implementation project, I encountered a familiar yet significant dilemma: determining the right approach for persistent data storage in a custom RICEF development. The functional team specifically requested something as intuitive and user-friendly as SM30 for data maintenance—a scenario many of us in the SAP ecosystem can relate to.

While the need for a custom configuration table was clear, the abundance of options in S/4HANA—across both on-premise and cloud environments—made the decision more complex than expected. Add to that the challenge of balancing legacy ECC practices with modern S/4HANA architecture, and the decision-making process becomes even more nuanced.

In this blog, I’ll walk through our real-world scenario, the solutions we considered, and the reasoning behind the final choice. The goal is to offer a concise yet practical guide to help others facing similar decisions in their S/4HANA journey.

Understanding the Landscape: On-Premise vs. Cloud

Before we dive into the specifics, it's crucial to understand that the choice of approach is heavily influenced by your SAP S/4HANA deployment model: on-premise or cloud. The"clean core" strategy, which emphasizes upgrade stability and limiting direct modifications to the core system, significantly shapes the available options, especially in the public cloud. What might be a viable solution on-premise could be entirely unsupported or not recommended in the cloud.

Let's break down the options by deployment model.

S/4HANA On-Premise: A Blend of Tradition and Modernity
In an S/4HANA on-premise environment, you have the flexibility to leverage both traditional ABAP development paradigms and newer, cloud-aligned approaches. This often leads to the dilemma that we faced: which path to take?

1. The Classical ABAP Approach: The Tried and True (with a Caveat)


This is perhaps the most familiar territory for many seasoned ABAP developers. It involves creating custom tables directly in the ABAP Dictionary (transaction SE11) and then generating a maintenance dialog using the Table Maintenance Generator (TMG) viaSE11 or SE54. End-users can then maintain data using the ubiquitous transaction SM30. You can even create custom transaction codes (SE93) for direct access, enhancing user experience and security. TMG also offers "events" where you can embed custom ABAP logic for validations, defaulting values, and other business requirements.

Features:

  • Full Control: You get granular control over table design, field properties, and database attributes. This is ideal for highly complex data models or scenarios requiring specific performance optimizations at the database level.
  • Familiarity: SM30 is a standard and widely known transaction, making it familiar to many SAP users for data maintenance.
  • Custom Logic: TMG events provide hooks for embedding custom ABAP logic, allowing for robust validations and business rules.

When to Use:

  • When maximum control and flexibility over table design and implementation are paramount.
  • For highly complex data models or specific performance optimizations that can only be achieved at the ABAP Dictionary level.
  • When existing SAP GUI-based processes are acceptable or preferred by the end-users, or when the development effort for a Fiori UI is not justified for the specific use case.

2. The Custom TVARVC Table Approach: Simple Configurations, Simple Maintenance

This approach involves creating a custom table (typically starting with 'Z' or 'Y') that mimics the structure of the standard TVARVC table. This custom table is then used to store application-specific parameters or simple configuration values. Maintenance is usually performed via Custom SAP GUI report/ transactions similar to STVARV or SM30.

Features:

  • Simple Configuration: Ideal for storing key-value pairs or simple ranges, such as company codes, plant-specific parameters, threshold values, or lists for custom logic. It's excellent for preventing hardcoding of values in ABAP programs, userexits, and reports.
  • Reusable Framework: A single custom TVARVC-style table and its supporting maintenance report can be designed as a reusable framework, enabling consistent configuration handling across multiple custom developments.

When to Use:

  • For simple configuration parameters that control custom ABAP developments.
  • When a quick and straightforward solution with minimal initial development for table structure and maintenance UI is needed.
  • When end-users are comfortable with SAP GUI for configuration maintenance.

3. Custom Business Objects (CBOs): The Modern On-Premise Approach

Custom Business Objects (CBOs) represent a modern and increasingly recommended method for defining custom data structures and behaviors in S/4HANA on-premise. They are managed via the "Custom Business Objects" Fiori app. A significant advantage of CBOs is the automatic generation of a Fiori-based UI for data maintenance, eliminating the need for manual UI development for basic CRUD operations. Each CBO is backed by a system-provisioned database table and inherently exposes an OData service for seamless integration with Fiori applications and other systems. This is essentially a low-code approach to custom tables with integrated Fiori maintenance.

Features:

  • Automatic Fiori UI: The system automatically generates a Fiori-based UI for data maintenance, providing a modern user experience with minimal development effort.
  • OData Service Exposure: CBOs automatically expose an OData service, simplifying integration with other Fiori apps, SAP systems, and external applications.
  • Key User Extensibility: CBOs empower key users to define and manage their owndata structures through a Fiori interface, reducing reliance on traditional ABAP developers for simple requirements.
  • Associations and Logic: Supports associations with other business objects for intricate data models and allows for custom logic (validations, determinations) via cloud BAdIs.

When to Use:

  • When the primary requirement is a user-friendly, modern Fiori interface for custom table maintenance.
  • For rapid development and deployment of maintenance UIs with minimal coding.
  • Well-suited for moderately complex data structures.
  • When you want to empower key users with extensibility capabilities.

4. Fiori Elements with ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model(RAP): The Future of On-Premise Development

This is a powerful and increasingly recommended approach for building Fiori applications, including those for custom table maintenance, in S/4HANA on-premise.RAP provides a standardized way to build OData services for transactional and analytical applications. Fiori Elements then automatically generate UIs based on these OData services and CDS views, significantly accelerating development.

Features:

  • Model-Driven UI: Fiori Elements generate UIs automatically from CDS views andOData services, drastically reducing manual UI coding.
  • RAP for Backend Logic: RAP handles the transactional and analytical logic,including CRUD operations for custom tables, ensuring a robust and scalablebackend.
  • Integrated Development: Development is done in ADT (ABAP Development Toolsin Eclipse), providing a modern development experience.

When to Use:

  • When building new Fiori applications for custom table maintenance in S/4HANA on-premise.
  • For scenarios requiring a robust, scalable, and performant solution that aligns with SAP's strategic direction.
  • When you want to leverage the benefits of automated UI generation and a consistent Fiori user experience.

5. Freestyle SAPUI5 Applications: Ultimate UI Customization


For scenarios requiring highly customized user interfaces or complex interactions not easily achievable with Fiori Elements or CBOs, developers can build freestyle SAPUI5applications. These applications consume OData services (often built using classical ABAP or RAP) that expose the custom table data.

Features:

  • Full UI Control: Developers have complete control over the UI layout, controls, and behavior, allowing for unique branding, complex data entry forms, or highly interactive dashboards.
  • Flexibility: Allows for highly specific UI/UX requirements that go beyond standard Fiori Elements templates.
  • Data Binding: Connects to backend data via OData services, which can be built using classical ABAP (SEGW) or RAP.

When to Use:

  • When your UI/UX requirements are extremely unique, complex, or cannot be met by standard Fiori Elements or CBO-generated UIs.
  • For highly interactive applications that require a bespoke user experience.

On-Premise Summary: Choosing the Right Persistent Storage Method

   

ApproachUI TypeWho Builds Coding RequiredClean Core AlignmentBest Use Case
Classical ABAP (SM30)SAP GUI (SM30)DevelopersMedium❌LowLegacy compatibility, complex tables, TMG event handling
Custom TVARVC TableSAP GUI (STVARV/SM30)DevelopersLow❌LowSimple config parameters, fast implementation
Custom Business Objects (CBOs)Fiori (Auto-generated)Key Users / DevsLow to Medium✅HighRapid deployment of Fiori-based maintenance apps
RAP + Fiori ElementsFiori (Auto-generated)DevelopersMedium to High✅HighModern architecture, scalable, maintainable development
Freestyle SAPUI5 AppFiori (Custom UI)DevelopersHigh✅High Highly customized UI/UX, complex workflows or dashboards

 

S/4HANA Cloud: The "Clean Core" Paradigm

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition), custom development follows strict “clean core” principles—minimizing direct system modifications to ensure upgrade stability. Traditional ABAP methods like SM30 or TVARVC-based tables are not applicable due to the absence of SAP GUI and limited backend extensibility.

Instead, SAP provides modern, cloud-compliant extensibility options:

1. Custom Business Objects (CBOs): Key User-Friendly and Fiori-Ready

CBOs are the recommended way to handle custom data needs in the cloud. Created via the "Custom Business Objects" Fiori app, they support:

  • Auto-generated Fiori UI for maintenance (CRUD)

  • Automatic OData exposure for integration

  • No-code/low-code development suited for business users

  • Upgrade-safe by design

Use When: You need a quick, compliant way to store and maintain custom data with minimal developer effort.

2. ABAP Cloud (Embedded Steampunk) + RAP: For Pro Developers

For advanced scenarios, ABAP Cloud (via Embedded Steampunk) allows professional developers to build applications using RAP within ADT. These apps:

  • Use whitelisted APIs and restricted ABAP syntax

  • Expose OData services for consumption in Fiori Elements

  • Are fully aligned with clean core standards

Use When: You need full control over backend logic or have complex business scenarios beyond what key user tools offer.

3. Custom SAPUI5 on BTP: For Bespoke UI Requirements

For highly customized or interactive UIs, build SAPUI5 apps on SAP BTP, consuming OData services from RAP or CBOs.

Use When: Standard Fiori templates don’t meet your UX needs and you want to keep extensions side-by-side (not inside the core).

Summary

     
ApproachUI TypeWho BuildsCoding RequiredClean Core ComplianceUse Case
CBOAuto Fiori UIKey UsersNo/Low✅HighSimple to moderate custom data needs
RAP + Fiori ElementsAuto Fiori UIDevelopersYes✅HighComplex business logic, developer-led
SAPUI5 on BTPCustom UIDevelopersYes✅Very High (Side-by-side)Bespoke UX or standalone apps

 

Making the Right Choice: A Decision Framework

Choosing the right approach for persistent data storage in your custom RICEF development is a critical decision that impacts development effort, user experience,integration capabilities, performance, security, transportability, and long-term maintainability. To simplify this, consider the following decision framework:

1. Deployment Model First: Are you on S/4HANA On-Premise or S/4HANA Cloud(Public Edition)? This is the most crucial differentiator. Cloud environments are far more restrictive due to the "clean core" strategy.

2. User Experience Requirement: Do your users require a traditional SAP GUI experience (less common now), a modern Fiori app experience, or a highly customized, bespoke UI?

3. Data Model Complexity: Is your custom data simple (key-value pairs), moderately complex (multiple fields, simple associations), or highly complex (intricate relationships, specific performance needs)?

4. Development Effort & Skills: What are your team's existing skills (classical ABAP,RAP, SAPUI5, BTP) and what is the available development budget and timeline?

5. "Clean Core" Adherence: How strictly do you need to adhere to the "clean core"principles for future upgrades and cloud readiness?

Here's a simplified table to guide your decision:

 

Feature/ConsiderationClassical ABAP(On-Premise)Custom TVARVC (On-Premise)CBOs (On-Premise &Cloud)RAP/Fiori Elements(On-Premise &Cloud)FreestyleUI5 (On-Premise &Cloud)
DeploymentOn-PremiseOn-PremiseOn-Premise &CloudOn-Premise &CloudOn-Premise &Cloud
UI Type
SAP		SAP GUI(SM30)SAP GUI(STVARV/SM30)Fiori(Auto-generated)Fiori(Auto-generated)Fiori(Custom)
Data ComplexityHighLowMediumHighHigh
Dev EffortMediumLowMediumMediumHigh
"Clean Core"AlignmentLowLowHighHighHigh(especially via BTP)
Key User ExtensibilityNoNoYesNo(Developer)No(Developer)
OData ExposureManual(SEGW)NoAutomaticAutomaticConsumes OData
Best ForLegacy, complex DB needsSimple configRapid Fiori appsRobust Fiori apps,complex logicUnique UI/UX needs

Final Thoughts

The world of SAP S/4HANA is constantly evolving, and so too are the best practices for custom development. The days of a one-size-fits-all approach are long gone. As ABAP developers and consultants, it's our responsibility to guide our functional colleagues and project teams towards the most appropriate and future-proof solutions.

My recent experience reminded me that while the technical options are abundant, the real challenge lies in understanding the business requirement, the system landscape,and the long-term implications of each choice. By carefully considering the factors outlined above, you can navigate this labyrinth with confidence, ensuring your custom RICEF developments are not only functional but also scalable, maintainable, and aligned with SAP's strategic direction.

Happy coding, and may your custom tables always be clean and performant!
